name: Homemade Catsup
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine tomatoes and onion in two large kettles. Bring to a boil; reduce heat, and simmer, uncovered, for 45 minutes, stirring frequently.
2. Remove from heat; put tomato mixture through a food mill or sieve, reserving tomato juice. Set aside.
3. Tie cinnamon sticks, cloves, and garlic in a cheesecloth bag; add to vinegar in a small saucepan. Bring to a boil; reduce heat, and simmer, uncovered, for 30 minutes.
4. Remove spice bag; set spiced vinegar aside.
5. Cook reserved tomato juice, uncovered, in a large kettle over medium-high heat for 2 hours or until volume is reduced by half, stirring frequently.
6. Add vinegar and remaining ingredients to the cooked tomato juice. Cook, uncovered, for 30-40 minutes, or until thickened.
7. Quickly pour hot mixture into hot jars, leaving 1/2-inch headspace. Remove air bubbles; wipe jar rims.
8. Cover with metal lids and screw on bands. Process in boiling-water bath for 20 minutes.
Yield: 6 pints.
